vocab.word: a chip off the old block
vocab.meaning: người có tính cách/sở thích giống hệt cha mẹ
vocab.example: Many young collectors buy expensive timepieces simply because they are a chip off the old block, inheriting their father's love for luxury watches.

vocab.meaning: sự kết hợp hoàn hảo giữa hai ưu điểm khác nhau
vocab.example: An expensive hybrid watch offers the best of both worlds: the prestige of a mechanical movement and the convenience of modern tech.

vocab.meaning: tăng vọt, cực kỳ cao (thường nói về giá cả hoặc nhu cầu)
vocab.example: The prices for certain vintage models have gone through the roof, leading people to wear them as a display of immense wealth.

vocab.word: in a league of their own
vocab.meaning: ở một đẳng cấp hoàn toàn khác biệt, không ai sánh kịp
vocab.example: High-end Swiss brands are in a league of their own when it comes to precision, which justifies the cost for many enthusiasts.

vocab.meaning: phải trả một cái giá quá đắt
vocab.example: Some are willing to pay through the nose for a limited edition piece just to ensure they have something truly unique on their wrist.

vocab.word: hit the nail on the head
vocab.meaning: nói đúng trọng tâm/chính xác vấn đề
vocab.example: If you say people buy luxury watches for the heritage, you've hit the nail on the head; the history of the brand is a huge selling point.

vocab.meaning: giữ cho ai đó luôn nắm bắt được thông tin/xu hướng
vocab.example: Wearing the latest model keeps a professional in the loop with the style standards of their high-ranking peers.

vocab.meaning: một sự thành công hay hiện tượng nhất thời (không bền vững)
vocab.example: Unlike smartwatches, which might be a flash in the pan, expensive mechanical watches are viewed as enduring investments.

vocab.meaning: một cách hoàn hảo, chính xác đến từng chi tiết
vocab.example: Luxury watches are finished down to a T, with every single component hand-polished to meet exacting standards.
In elite social circles, one's accessories must be up to par, and a high-end watch is often the expected benchmark.
